title: Can we imagine a release of the master branch ?
author: fwininger
body: Can we imagine a release of the master branch ?

title: Problem with "default" queue
author: JerrodCarpenter
body:
Hello, our "default" queue is "within_1_minute". I noticed after making this transition, sidekiq-cron continued to enqueue things on the old "default" queue (default). I noticed [this](https://github.com/sidekiq-cron/sidekiq-cron/issues/512) older issue that seems related, but looks to have fixed it at least partially? We are not using active job.

 Versions:
* sidekiq-cron (2.3.1)
* sidekiq (8.0.10)
* rails (7.2.3)

title: Reflected XSS on the Sidekiq-UI
author: fwininger
body:
# Summary ## XSS Vulnerability Specially crafted GET request parameters handled by any of the following endpoints of `sidekiq-cron` "admin" web UI, allow a super-user attacker, or an unwitting, but authorized, victim, who has received a disguised / crafted link, to successfully execute malicious code, which could potentially steal cookies, session data, or local storage data from the app the `sidekiq-cron` web UI is mounted in. # Details The vulnerability is a deliberate XSS attack. In the latest versions of Sidekiq, the vulnerability is mitigated by CSPs. However, within the framework of a defense-in-depth strategy, it seems essential to fix the vulnerability at its source. # PoC With a standard rails application with the sidekiq and sidekiq-cron install go to : ``` http://localhost:3000/sidekiq/cron/namespaces/

hello

``` Note : This vulnerability is mitigated by the CSPs introduced in version 7.3.0, see [change here](https://github.com/sidekiq/sidekiq/blob/main/Changes.md#730). Without the fix (main branch) : With the fix on the #568 :

title: Make the polling interval more configurable
author: kevinrobell-st
body:
We currently run only two processes with Sidekiq Cron to avoid polling Redis too often. This works fine for our use case. Unexpectedly the number of processes seems to affect how the polling period is calculated in Sidekiq.

The polling period is calculated here:
https://github.com/sidekiq/sidekiq/blob/e03b317f2070655c51fad838b0ecfb99c6d6f853/lib/sidekiq/scheduled.rb#L129-L160

The `poll_interval_average` method gets overridden here:
https://github.com/sidekiq-cron/sidekiq-cron/blob/8c25ca29f5259cdd5a29fe8f38434eff1445dbb7/lib/sidekiq/cron/poller.rb#L43-L45

Let's say the `cron_poll_interval` is set to 5 seconds. If the number of processes is more than 10, it will poll every 0.0-5.0 seconds or every 2.5 seconds on average. If the number of processes is less than or equal to 10, it will poll every 2.5-7.5 seconds or every 5 seconds on average.

It would be nice to have a way to override the `process_count` to reflect the actual number of processes running Sidekiq Cron or provide a more flexible way to specify the polling period interval.

title: Suggestion:  Clarify time on web UI
author: vanboom
body:
We are switching over from `sidecloq` and had a suggestion...

On the web UI, it is not clear if the schedule works in UTC or local time so a suggestion would be to add the "current time" to the top of the web UI in the same zone as the schedule.  The user would then see for example "At 10:00 every day" and "Current time: 09:53".  

We also had a fork of `sidecloq` that would show distance of time in words in the schedule table as a helper to the viewer, e.g. "0 12 * * * | Next run in about 12 minutes". 

Thanks for your consideration.

title: Sidekiq web fails if sideloaded
author: addam128
body:
Hola,
I have run into a small issue where  the sidekiq-cron web fails to load when the sidekiq-web is run from a different script than the job server. The problem lies in requiring `sidkekiq/cron/web`  as it  avoids loading the  [cron file](https://github.com/sidekiq-cron/sidekiq-cron/blob/master/lib/sidekiq/cron.rb). Therefore, calls to `Sidekiq::Cron.configuration` fail with an undefined method error.

The fix is simple, you need `require'sidekiq-cron'` before requiring the web, but it would be nice to either mention this in the readme or load the main file from the `web.rb`.

title: Doesn't work with sidekiq enterprise
author: optikalefx
body:
We have sidekiq enterprise which has a "periodic" cron system that runs at startup. I installed this gem to dynamically create CRON jobs. Even though the cron job objects do get created (I can find them with Sidekiq::Cron::Job.find, they don't actually run.

Do you know why there would be incompatibility between this gem and sidekiq enterprise, and what it might take to make it work? I can work on this, but I don't quite know the setup for getting started.

- ruby 3.4.3

title: `Sidekiq::Cron::Job` transforms `args` for json string representing a hash to an array with key-value
author: francktrouillez
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a `Sidekiq::Cron::Job` with `args` being a json representing an object `{ "my_key" => "my_value" }.to_json`
2. Assign it to a variable (`job` for instance)
3. Observe that the `job#args` returns parsed `args` :arrow_right: `job.args == { "my_key" => "my_value" } # => true`
4. Enqueue the job with `job.enqueue!`
5. Observe that the `args` passed to the worker is an array representing key-value :arrow_right: `worker.args == ["my_key", "my_value"] # => true`

Here is a reproducible code snippet.

_Note: I've added a redis docker container setup for redis to be available for the test to run, but feel free to tweak it the way you want. This obviously assumes you have docker installed and available in your PATH._

```ruby
# frozen_string_literal: true

# Setup a running Redis Docker container for Sidekiq testing.
# Assumes Docker is installed and available in the PATH.
REDIS_NAME_CONTAINER = "redis-test-sidekiq"
started_redis = false
if system("which docker > /dev/null 2>&1")
  container = `docker ps -q -f name=#{REDIS_NAME_CONTAINER}`.strip
  if container.empty?
    container_exists = `docker ps -a -q -f name=#{REDIS_NAME_CONTAINER}`.strip
    if container_exists.empty?
      puts "Starting a new Redis Docker container..."
      # Run a new Redis container mapped to the host's 6379 port.
      if system("docker run -d -p 6379:6379 --name #{REDIS_NAME_CONTAINER} redis:latest > /dev/null 2>&1")
        puts "Redis Docker container started."
        started_redis = true
      else
        puts "Failed to start Redis Docker container. Exiting..."
        exit 1
      end
    else
      puts "Starting existing Redis Docker container..."
      if system("docker start #{REDIS_NAME_CONTAINER} > /dev/null 2>&1")
        puts "Redis Docker container started."
        started_redis = true
      else
        puts "Failed to start existing Redis Docker container. Exiting..."
        exit 1
      end
    end
  else
    puts "Redis Docker container is already running."
  end
else
  puts "Docker command not found. Make sure Docker is installed."
end

# Ensure that we stop and remove the Redis container when the script exits.
at_exit do
  if started_redis
    system("docker stop #{REDIS_NAME_CONTAINER} > /dev/null 2>&1")
    system("docker rm #{REDIS_NAME_CONTAINER} > /dev/null 2>&1")
    puts "Stopped and removed Redis Docker container."
  end
end

# Load the required gems and run the test.

require "bundler/inline"

gemfile(true) do
  source "https://rubygems.org"

  gem "sidekiq"
  gem "sidekiq-cron", git: "https://github.com/sidekiq-cron/sidekiq-cron.git"
end

puts "Ruby version: #{RUBY_VERSION}"
puts "Sidekiq version: #{Sidekiq::VERSION}"
puts "Sidekiq-Cron version: #{Sidekiq::Cron::VERSION}"

require "minitest/autorun"
require "sidekiq/testing"
Sidekiq::Testing.inline!

class MyWorker
  include Sidekiq::Worker

  def perform(args); self.class.args = args; end
  def self.args=(args); @args = args; end
  def self.args; @args; end
end

class BugTest < Minitest::Test
  def test_bug
    name = "test"
    args = { "my_key" => "my_value" }.to_json
    Sidekiq::Cron::Job.create(
      name: name,
      cron: "0 0 * * *",
      class: MyWorker,
      args: args
    )
    job = Sidekiq::Cron::Job.find(name)
    job.enqueue!

    assert_equal job.args, JSON.parse(args) # Success
    assert_equal MyWorker.args, JSON.parse(args) # Failure
  end
end

```

### Expected behavior
<!-- Tell us what should happen -->
Expect `MyWorker.args` to be equal to `JSON.parse(args)`.

This is assuming we are ok with the behavior of `job.args` being parsed to a hash, even though I gave a string. (that can be another discussion, out of scope for this issue imo)

### Actual behavior
<!-- Tell us what happens instead -->
`MyWorker.args` and `JSON.parse(args)` are not the same:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/15f50710-cd9a-45d2-9ba8-4d1375bdec08)

`MyWorker.args` is instead an array representing a key-value pair: `["my_key", "my_value"]`.

This means that when we give as `args` a string `"{\"my_key\":\"my_value\"}"`, it in fact leads to an array `["my_key", "my_value"]` being given to our worker, which to me is quite unexpected.

Ideally, I would expect that the `args` I pass to the `Sidekiq::Cron::Job#initialize` is actually what is passed to the `perform_async` method of the given worker.

### System configuration
**Ruby version**: 3.2.3
**Sidekiq version**: 8.0.2
**Sidekiq-Cron version**: 2.2.0

### Closing thoughts
If this behavior is indeed not expected, I can try to dig in to produce a PR to fix it.

Thanks for your time and for the great work you do with this gem!

title: LocalJumpError when embedding sidekiq on 2.2.0
author: rafal-brize
body:
Hello i tried to bump to 2.2.0

And in our case we are embedding sidekiq within puma process, following

https://github.com/sidekiq/sidekiq/wiki/Embedding#puma

On 2.2.0 we get 

`WARNING hook before_worker_boot failed with exception (LocalJumpError) break from proc-closure`

which i pinpointed to 

https://github.com/sidekiq-cron/sidekiq-cron/blob/master/lib/sidekiq/cron/schedule_loader.rb#L53

The fix that makes it work is to you change `break` to `next` as 

configure_embed is calling those procs as follows

https://github.com/sidekiq/sidekiq/blob/68b5462fb0a0a1a0299acb2edc0c7cff11e60f71/lib/sidekiq.rb#L130

and `break` stops the iterations

Please @markets can you confirm my reasoning is sound?

title: 'invalid date' error when parsing last_enqueued_time
author: marcogregorius
body:
We've encountered an issue with `sidekiq-cron` v1.12.0 where invalid dates are being generated, causing Sidekiq to fail during initialization. This issue is preventing our application (GitLab) from starting up properly. Bug link on GitLab: https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/issues/523081

**Observed Behavior:**

1. Sidekiq fails to initialize due to an 'invalid date' error.
2. The error occurs when trying to parse the `last_enqueue_time` for cron jobs.
3. Specifically, we've seen instances where the date `2025-02-29` from `last_enqueue_time` is being generated, which is invalid as 2025 is not a leap year.

**Error logs:**

* Sidekiq logs showed usual initialisation messages, such as:

  ```
  {"severity":"INFO","time":"YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.000Z","message":"Sidekiq x.y.z connecting to Redis with ptions ...
  ```

  followed by:

  ```
  invalid date
  ```
* The top of the stack trace:

  ```
  /opt/gitlab/embedded/lib/ruby/gems/3.1.0/gems/sidekiq-cron-1.12.0/lib/sidekiq/cron/job.rb:631:in `parse'
  /opt/gitlab/embedded/lib/ruby/gems/3.1.0/gems/sidekiq-cron-1.12.0/lib/sidekiq/cron/job.rb:631:in `rescue in parse_enqueue_time'
  /opt/gitlab/embedded/lib/ruby/gems/3.1.0/gems/sidekiq-cron-1.12.0/lib/sidekiq/cron/job.rb:628:in `parse_enqueue_time'
  /opt/gitlab/embedded/lib/ruby/gems/3.1.0/gems/sidekiq-cron-1.12.0/lib/sidekiq/cron/job.rb:290:in `initialize'
  /opt/gitlab/embedded/lib/ruby/gems/3.1.0/gems/sidekiq-cron-1.12.0/lib/sidekiq/cron/job.rb:228:in `new'
  /opt/gitlab/embedded/lib/ruby/gems/3.1.0/gems/sidekiq-cron-1.12.0/lib/sidekiq/cron/job.rb:228:in `block in all'
  /opt/gitlab/embedded/lib/ruby/gems/3.1.0/gems/sidekiq-cron-1.12.0/lib/sidekiq/cron/job.rb:226:in `collect'
  /opt/gitlab/embedded/lib/ruby/gems/3.1.0/gems/sidekiq-cron-1.12.0/lib/sidekiq/cron/job.rb:226:in `all'
  /opt/gitlab/embedded/lib/ruby/gems/3.1.0/gems/sidekiq-cron-1.12.0/lib/sidekiq/cron/job.rb:534:in `destroy_removed_jobs'
  /opt/gitlab/embedded/lib/ruby/gems/3.1.0/gems/sidekiq-cron-1.12.0/lib/sidekiq/cron/job.rb:177:in `load_from_hash!'
  /opt/gitlab/embedded/service/gitlab-rails/config/initializers/sidekiq.rb:9:in `load_cron_jobs!'
  /opt/gitlab/embedded/service/gitlab-rails/config/initializers/sidekiq.rb:113:in `block in <top (required)>'
  /opt/gitlab/embedded/service/gitlab-rails/vendor/gems/sidekiq-7.1.6/lib/sidekiq.rb:98:in `configure_server'
  /opt/gitlab/embedded/service/gitlab-rails/config/initializers/sidekiq.rb:41:in `<top (required)>'
  /opt/gitlab/embedded/lib/ruby/gems/3.1.0/gems/railties-7.0.8.1/lib/rails/engine.rb:667:in `load'
  /opt/gitlab/embedded/lib/ruby/gems/3.1.0/gems/railties-7.0.8.1/lib/rails/engine.rb:667:in `block in load_config_initializer'
  /opt/gitlab/embedded/lib/ruby/gems/3.1.0/gems/activesupport-7.0.8.1/lib/active_support/notifications.rb:208:in `instrument'
  ```

**Impact:**

* This issue causes a crash loop in our application, preventing it from starting up.
* It affects multiple customers across different time zones.

**Additional Information:**

* The issue seems to occur around date changes, particularly in certain time zones (e.g., JST, China Standard Time).
* The problem appears to be intermittent and difficult to reproduce in test environments.

**Questions:**

1. Is there a known issue with date handling in sidekiq-cron, particularly around month boundaries or leap year calculations?
2. Are there any recommended workarounds or fixes for this issue?

We appreciate any insights or assistance you can provide in resolving this critical issue. Let me know if there is additional information needed to help diagnose this problem. Thank you for your time and effort.

title: Redis DB memory usage increased after v2 upgrade
author: eileen-jiang
body:
After upgrading to v2, we noticed that our **Redis DB memory usage** went from a flat 1.6% in pre-v2 and started climbing over the course of the next 24 hrs to 2.4%, before we reverted back to pre-v2. 

Any idea why this is? Is v2 leaking Redis keys in any way?

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dcff5a94-14fe-4184-97ab-c46ee9ecbe7d)

title: Improve UI on Sidekiq v8
author: markets
body: We recently merged Sidekiq v8 support (https://github.com/sidekiq-cron/sidekiq-cron/pull/531), but the UI needs some love.

title: Dynamic arguments
author: krystof-k
body:
*Opening new issue based on #533.*

I would like to be able to pass dynamic arguments to a cron job, for example:

```yaml
Do something since:
  class: DoSomethingSinceJob
  args: [<%= 2.days.ago.to_s %>]
  cron: every day
```

Currently, this is not possible, because ERB is evaluated at the time of Sidekiq boot and all jobs would have the same argument value.

title: Cannot evaluate `Time` inside YAML

I think it should be generally safe to allow `Time` here:

https://github.com/sidekiq-cron/sidekiq-cron/blob/6fff355eb8701ec910d831412286c4244d81c5c9/lib/sidekiq/cron/support.rb#L37

title: Show parsed cron expression in web UI when used natural language
author: krystof-k
body:
Currently if natural language definition is used, it is rendered twice in the web UI:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/29795919-c958-4be7-813b-f35d38a199cd)

It would be better to render the parsed cron expression as the secondary `<small>` text in that case. I think it is this line:

https://github.com/sidekiq-cron/sidekiq-cron/blob/6fff355eb8701ec910d831412286c4244d81c5c9/lib/sidekiq/cron/views/cron.erb#L79

title: Sidekiq v8.0: undefined method 'settings' for class Sidekiq::Web::Application
author: denisj
body:
I have updated Sidekiq to v8.0.0, and I'm using sidekiq-cron 2.1.0. I get this issue:

`
undefined method 'settings' for class Sidekiq::Web::Application
`

on the line "require 'sidekiq/cron/web'"

```ruby
require 'sidekiq/web'
require 'sidekiq/cron/web'
mount Sidekiq::Web => '/sidekiq'
````

title: `.yaml` extension not loading
author: krystof-k
body: I just spent few minutes confused why the jobs are not scheduled and found out that the default YAML file name is `schedule.yml` and `schedule.yaml` is not loading. I understand it is just a default value, but it would be would be nice if `.yaml` also works, as it is [the official YAML extension](https://yaml.org/faq.html).
